message:
fix(messaging): enforce family-scoped subject patterns in ControlRegistry

ControlRegistry::register() previously only checked the $SYS.FABRIC.
prefix but did not verify that the pattern was actually scoped to the
declared SystemSubjectFamily. A handler registered under Health could
match AUTH subjects (e.g. $SYS.FABRIC.AUTH.>) or similar-prefix
subjects outside the family boundary (e.g. $SYS.FABRIC.HEALTHY.>).

Add FamilyMismatch error variant and family_prefix() boundary check:
the pattern must either equal the family prefix exactly or continue
with a dot separator. This prevents cross-family subject leakage.

Tests added:
- register_rejects_family_mismatch (Health handler with AUTH pattern)
- register_rejects_similar_prefix_outside_family_boundary (HEALTHY.>)
- unregister_one_break_glass_preserves_remaining_handlers
- family_scoped_pattern_routes_only_within_declared_prefix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
